name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: re: Washington Post editorial
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
The media's coverage of this election | Derek --

This morning, the _Washington Post_ editorial board took a moment to examine how Matt Lauer treated the two candidates at the forum on Wednesday night -- where a third of Hillary's time was spent answering questions on emails and Trump got asked softball questions he could answer with lies.

After acknowledging that fact, the _Post_ asked their readers to consider the following: [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MjY0NzAyNjES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNTI5NjgxS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Derek, this is the fundamental problem with the way the media at large covers the presidential race in 2016 -- [**and it's why we need the resources to get the facts in front of voters every day from now through November 8th.**](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjY0NzAyNjES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNTI5NjgxS0&mt=1&rt=0)

I work with reporters every day, Derek. I'll be the first to tell you how hard they work -- the extent to which they try to be objective, evenhanded, and fair.

But there's just one problem: They’ve never had to cover two candidates as vastly different as Hillary and Trump.

When cable news and major papers spend the same amount of time (or more!) covering Hillary's emails as they do covering Trump's ties to white supremacists, unapologetic admiration for Vladimir Putin, or blatant history of discrimination, voters begin to treat the two candidates as if they were equivalent.

And too many people come to see this election as a choice between “the lesser of two evils” instead of a stark decision between a gifted, dedicated public servant and a dangerous, divisive authoritarian.

So our biggest test as a team in the 60 days we have left is to try to accomplish what the media has so far failed to do, and make sure every voter understands how dangerous a Trump presidency would be. The world is counting on us.

**Getting it done and winning this election in spite of the forces fighting against us takes ads, organizers, and you, Derek. We’ll even send you a free sticker as a thank you if you can help us out for the first time right now:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjY0NzAyNjES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNTI5NjgxS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks,

Christina

Christina Reynolds
Deputy Communications Director
Hillary for America

|

name: Pat Murray (via VanHollen.org)
subject: following up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
|

Friend,

I wanted to send you a quick update as we hit the 60 day out mark in this U.S. Senate election.

Yesterday our campaign learned that Chris' Republican opponent released a signaling memo with instructions for big money groups like Citizens United to attack Chris and try to change the course of this race.

With an opponent in the pocket of special interests and big donors abandoning Trump's presidential campaign to focus on down ballot races nationwide, we don't know exactly what to expect. One thing is clear -- our people-powered campaign needs to be ready for whatever comes our way.

**[Maryland can't afford another November surprise. We need to be prepared. Please donate $25 now to let Chris know you've got his back and to ensure we have the funds to get the job done. ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ae1/70cdc2ba/4712f0be/39748a7/524656986/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em090916_1Re&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Maryland+canrsquot+afford+another+Novemb&utm_campaign=em090916_1&source=em090916_1")**

After crisscrossing the state this summer, Chris is back in session this week working to make sure the Tea Party doesn't orchestrate another government shutdown, pushing Republicans to permit a vote on banning terrorists from purchasing guns, and providing desperately needed funding to combat the Zika virus - among other things.

**[He's standing up for us. Now is the time to stand up for him. Chip in now>> ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91ae1/70cdc2ba/4712f0be/39748a4/524656986/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cvh-keep-fighting?refcode=em090916_1Re&amount=25.00&recurring=12&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Hersquos+standing+up+for+us+Now+is+the+t&utm_campaign=em090916_1&source=em090916_1")**

Thank you for standing with Chris.

Pat Murray
Campaign Manager
Van Hollen for Senate

name: Ben Carson
subject: Join Me Tomorrow w/ Michael W. Smith, Mike Huckabee, & Allen Jackson
body:
Untitled 1

Untitled 1

Stand, Pray, Give Thanks A Living
Hope Event

View this email in your browser

I can remember exactly where I
was on that fateful day of September 11, 2001. The events of that
day impacted my life and the lives of all Americans in ways we
could have never expected. As I reflect back on that day and the
fifteen years that have passed since, my heart is filled with
gratitude for all of the amazing first responders and volunteers
that not only came to the rescue that day, but have continued to
do so every day since.
I am honored to be a part of the
Give Thanks event this Sunday on 9/11 along with Pastor Allen
Jackson, Michael W. Smith, Mike Huckabee, Eric Metaxas, Rend
Collective, Charlie Daniels and others. My Faith Votes is
offering you a FREE and exclusive LIVE behind the scenes
perspective of the event via Facebook Live.

Join me and thousands of others
in an amazing evening of music, hope and thankfulness to God for
His blessings on this great nation.

subject: re: the number of Trump’s supporters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
He's gaining on us | Derek --

Trump’s campaign announced this week that about $70 million of what they raised in August came from small-dollar donors -- people who can donate again and again and again. And these folks are now part of a swelling Trump team of 2.1 million donors.

I'm going to be honest: That's a bigger number than any of us realized.

**[For the record, we have 2.3 million supporters on our team -- and we’re very proud of that.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjY0NzA1MjYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNTM1MzIyS0&mt=1&rt=0)** But we’ve been building this crew for well over a year. Trump started fundraising in a serious, wide-reaching way only a couple of months ago. He’s gaining on us fast, and he’s not slowing down.

With the kind of surging small-dollar support Trump now has behind him, he can consistently raise well over $70-$80 million each month just from these people.

And make no mistake, Trump can still write himself a big check whenever he needs. And of course there are untold sums of super PAC money spent attacking Hillary every day.

But we can account for all that. The thing that is starting to make me lose sleep is the money Trump is raising from millions of people who will stop at nothing to see him elected president.

We’ve had a couple of good days lately from a fundraising standpoint -- and that’s thanks solely to the dedication and focus of this team. But if we step back and look at the whole month of September, we're still behind on our overall goal. And that trend is our reality while Trump is supercharging his campaign.

This can't continue, Derek.

With 59 days to go until Election Day, we need every single person on our team to be invested, because that’s where Trump’s people are -- committed, passionate, and stepping up more and more. **We can’t afford to let them beat us in the last sprint of this race. Can you chip in for the first time to prove that we’re stronger and we’re going to win this? We’ll send you a free sticker as a personal thank you:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjY0NzA1MjYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNTM1MzIyS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you so much -- I know we can count on you.

Dennis

Dennis Cheng
National Finance Director
Hillary for America

|
**Hillary needs you now more than ever -- chip in $1 and join the 2.3 million grassroots donors standing with her:**
| [

Friend,
As National Spokesperson for the Trump for President campaign, I've had the privilege of getting to know Mr. Trump's children over these last few months.
And I can tell you Friend, _I 've seen firsthand just why America loves them so much._ They are all caring, intelligent, and down-to-earth people.
They would certainly make any father proud.
I've been particularly drawn to Ivanka, Mr. Trump's eldest daughter. She is an impressive young businesswoman, with elegance and poise that belies her age. Her speech at the Republican National Convention was proof of that.

**She is a truly amazing woman, and that 's why I'm so excited to announce a _once-in-a-lifetime_ opportunity for one of Mr. Trump's supporters to join Ivanka for coffee at Trump International Hotel & Tower.**
**[Make a donation of just $3 or more and your name will be automatically entered to win a free trip to New York City to join Ivanka Trump for coffee at Trump International Hotel& Tower.](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/eccbaK1yBABMUiBB8OoaEVLjDRMN6aaaaJKWsBL2IYUja?s=4@z70~19&c=fnygnjkq85_ikcgn.aqk&n=l&z=&p=273y34Aaqdhcg8ktclmYAir8vni8lde8v8v8jdAc&v8v)**

I can think of no greater testament to the greatness of Donald Trump than to look at the wonderful children he has raised.
And, I promise you, this is an opportunity to spend time with Ivanka is one _you will not want to miss._
Thank you for all of the support you've given Mr. Trump throughout his incredible and historic campaign for the White House.
**[Contribute just $3 or more today for your chance to win.](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/eccbaK1yBABMUiBB8OoaEVLjDRMN6aaaaJKWsBL2IYUja?s=4@z70~19&c=fnygnjkq85_ikcgn.aqk&n=n&z=&p=273y34Aaqdhcg8ktclmYAir8vni8lde8v8v8jdAc&v8v)**

Katrina Pierson

body:
Hello there,
Senator Elizabeth Warren is my kind of fighter. In Washington, she's stood up for the middle class against corporate special interests, and continues to push for campaign finance reform and defend women's health care rights. I can't wait to welcome her back to New Hampshire later this month, and I'd love for you and a friend to join us too.
[**Donate before tonight's deadline to automatically be entered for a chance to win and meet Senator Warren and me here in New Hampshire. We'll cover transportation and accommodations for you and a guest.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd89a/2a380d31/1174df20/fdec76/1055904656/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuLXdhcnJlbi1jb250ZXN0IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxNjA5MTBfd2FycmVuaGFzc2FuX3YyIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan-warren-contest?refcode=e20160910_warrenhassan_v2&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

I hope to see you soon.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

The responsibility shifts to us. | Derek --

Welcome to the media’s version of “normal” -- where Donald Trump gets graded on a curve just for showing up, and there's no sustained effort to press him on the questions that deserve real answers.

On Wednesday night, at the Commander-in-Chief Forum, Trump said he stood by the idea that sexual assault is the price you pay for allowing men and women to serve together in the military. The response from the media at large? Mostly a shrug. Any effort to contextualize those remarks got lost in discussion about optics from the event.

At that same event, Trump lied about his opposition to the Iraq war, and the moderator for the night, Matt Lauer, never pushed back. It was the perfect example of the media's inability to even fact-check this guy.

In a normal election, just one moment like this -- an example of such casual misogyny or another repeated, unblinking lie -- would be the topic of a news story for days. For Trump, that's not the case.

So the responsibility shifts to us.

It's on this team to give voters the information they need about how to define these two candidates, and to meet that obligation, we need to resources to do things like hire enough organizers to blanket the country and run the kind of advertising program it takes to cut through the noise.

[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/120246b/2fde7c66/40aa636d/156a99af/4004168614/VEsH/ "http://volunteer.roycooper.com/?utm_campaign=fld_sepvol_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em16_160910_fld_gq_sepvol_ful__mb")
---
Friends, We have the opportunity to make history. If we elect Roy in November, Governor McCrory will become the first governor to lose after one term in North Carolina's modern history. With less than two months before Election Day, we're going to do our best to make that happen. name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: One major problem in this election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
We must hold Trump accountable | Derek --

The last few days have offered a concrete example of one major problem in this election: the trouble the media has in holding Trump accountable -- on his lies or what we now understand to be clearcut examples of corruption.

Take, for example, the narrative around two foundations.

You've probably heard a lot about the Clinton Foundation in the past month. That's because dedicated teams of reporters from news outlets all over the country have devoted major time and resources to unearthing some examples of wrongdoing in the charity's past. And the grand total of all those investigations? Nothing but confirmation that the foundation does unassailable, lifesaving work in countries across the globe.

On the other hand, you have the Trump Foundation. Back in 2013, they donated $25,000 to a committee associated with Pam Bondi’s re-election as Florida attorney general. At the time, Bondi's office was considering whether or not to join a lawsuit against Trump University. But after Bondi received that contribution, her office decided not to join the lawsuit. Bondi subsequently endorsed Trump's bid for president, then spoke at the Republican National Convention -- where she said Hillary Clinton should go to jail. Trump’s foundation also gave $100,000 to Citizens United to sue New York’s attorney general—after he’d started investigating Trump University.

This is blockbuster stuff, Derek -- but months after that story came out, there's been little sustained effort from the press to force Trump to address the charge that he bought off the top law enforcement official in Florida. And in the 59 days we have left in this election, we shouldn't expect such an effort to begin.

**[So holding Trump accountable is on our collective shoulders.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjY0NzM4MDIS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNjI5NTc0S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

We're the team who has to give voters the information they need about the two candidates, and that means we’re going to have to hire enough organizers to blanket the country with volunteers and run ads on television and the internet often enough to make a real impression.

**So we need 5 donations in your area to make sure we can reach the voters we’ll need -- will you make one of those donations right now?** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjY0NzM4MDIS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgxNjI5NTc0S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Christina

Deputy Communications Director
Hillary for America

clean_body: We must hold Trump accountable | Derek -- The last few days have offered a concrete example of one major problem in this election: the trouble the media has in holding Trump accountable -- on his lies or what we now understand to be clearcut examples of corruption. Take, for example, the narrative around two foundations. You've probably heard a lot about the Clinton Foundation in the past month. That's because dedicated teams of reporters from news outlets all over the country have devoted major time and resources to unearthing some examples of wrongdoing in the charity's past. And the grand total of all those investigations? Nothing but confirmation that the foundation does unassailable, lifesaving work in countries across the globe. On the other hand, you have the Trump Foundation. Back in 2013, they donated $25,000 to a committee associated with Pam Bondi’s re-election as Florida attorney general. At the time, Bondi's office was considering whether or not to join a lawsuit against Trump University. But after Bondi received that contribution, her office decided not to join the lawsuit. Bondi subsequently endorsed Trump's bid for president, then spoke at the Republican National Convention -- where she said Hillary Clinton should go to jail. Trump’s foundation also gave $100,000 to Citizens United to sue New York’s attorney general—after he’d started investigating Trump University. This is blockbuster stuff, Derek -- but months after that story came out, there's been little sustained effort from the press to force Trump to address the charge that he bought off the top law enforcement official in Florida. And in the 59 days we have left in this election, we shouldn't expect such an effort to begin. We're the team who has to give voters the information they need about the two candidates, and that means we’re going to have to hire enough organizers to blanket the country with volunteers and run ads on television and the internet often enough to make a real impression. So we need 5 donations in your area to make sure we can reach the voters we’ll need -- will you make one of those donations right now?
